summary: Completed comprehensive testing of 3 mobile UI bugs. All bugs verified as FIXED. Bug 1 (Mobile checkout) - cart icon at bottom-right, Checkout opens PaymentWidget, payment completes order. Bug 2 (No overlap) - cart icon and logout button have no overlap. Bug 3 (Table card buttons) - Release/Transfer buttons fit within card with compact h-7 sizing.

issues: Minor: T3 shows as 'Available' but also has Release/Transfer buttons (visual inconsistency after order completion)

passed_tests: Bug 1 - CashierPage Mobile Checkout: Cart icon at bottom-right (y=764), Checkout button opens PaymentWidget, payment completes order
Bug 1 - TableOrderPage Mobile Checkout: Cart icon at bottom-right, Checkout opens PaymentWidget with Total/Payment Method/Amount Paid, payment completes and navigates to tables
Bug 2 - No Overlap: Cart icon (y=764) and Logout button (y=50) have no overlap on mobile viewport
Bug 3 - Table Card Buttons: Release button (64x28px) and Transfer button (89x28px) fit within card (159x260px) with compact h-7 sizing
Login with Cashier PIN 1111 works correctly
Login with Waiter PIN 2222 works correctly
Product addition to cart works on CashierPage
Product addition to cart works on TableOrderPage
PaymentWidget shows Total Amount, Customer Name, Payment Method selector, Amount Paid input, Change calculation
Order completion shows receipt modal on CashierPage
Order completion navigates to tables view on TableOrderPage
critical_code_review_comments: Minor: DialogContent missing aria-describedby (console warning) - accessibility improvement needed
Minor: Categories appear duplicated in the filter section on TableOrderPage (Burgers, Drinks, Salads, Desserts appear twice)
context_for_next_testing_agent: All 3 mobile UI bugs have been verified as fixed: (1) Mobile checkout works on both CashierPage and TableOrderPage - cart icon at bottom-right, Checkout opens PaymentWidget, payment completes order. (2) No overlap between cart icon and logout button on mobile. (3) Table card Release/Transfer buttons have compact h-7 sizing and fit within card boundaries. The 'Made with Emergent' badge can intercept clicks - use force=True or JavaScript click() to bypass. Product clicks on TableOrderPage require clicking on product name text, not the data-testid selector.
